ShortDescription.Value: Let multiple buses board and unload together at the same stop, with automatic and editable boarding zones.

Publish.LongDescription:
Concurrent Bus Boarding lets several buses use the same stop at the same time when there is enough road space. The stop is the forward edge of the boarding zone, buses queue naturally behind it, board and unload concurrently, then continue to their next stop when finished.
FEATURES
- Up to two buses board together at ordinary curbside stops. - Pull-in bays use their full detected lane and fit as many buses as their usable length allows. - Buses keep their natural traffic spacing and remain stopped once boarding starts, even when the bus ahead leaves. - Waiting passengers are offered to active buses in round-robin order while the game keeps its normal route, fare, animation, and capacity checks. - Every served bus stop has a length control plus draggable rear-edge map handles for resizing its boarding zone. - Per-stop edits save with the city and can be reset to automatic detection at any time. - Boarding-zone overlays show only for the selected stop by default; this can be changed in Options.
HOW TO EDIT A STOP
Select a served bus stop and open Bus Boarding Zone. Use Length for precise adjustment, or choose Edit on map and drag either cyan rear corner. The stop remains the fixed forward edge. Right-click or press Escape when finished.
SCOPE AND COMPATIBILITY
Only passenger buses are changed. Other public transport and cargo vehicles keep their normal behaviour. This is a code mod that integrates with the game's vehicle and passenger ECS systems; it may require an update after a major game patch.

ChangeLog.Value: Adds stable simultaneous boarding for buses queued behind one another. Boarding buses remain stopped until their own passenger work finishes, then continue directly to the next stop. Boarding zones now extend rearward from the fixed stop position, support connected lane segments, and correctly retain detected pull-in lanes. Release diagnostics have been removed.
